recommendations: Add a minimal smoke-test suite for generated MCP endpoints (import
call
and schema validation) and wire it into CI
Filter exposed endpoints to public/stable API only (avoid private underscored methods by default) with an explicit allowlist for internals
Define typed request/response Pydantic models per endpoint (especially Graph/Vertex/Edge serialization) to prevent ambiguous payloads
Introduce robust object-handle/session management for graph objects instead of passing large raw structures between calls
Add structured error mapping (ValueError/TypeError/IOError -> MCP-friendly error codes/messages) with consistent diagnostics
Implement timeout and resource guards for expensive graph algorithms/layouts plus configurable execution limits
Add deterministic options for layout/randomized algorithms (seed support surfaced in MCP) to improve reproducibility
Create integration tests for optional backends (matplotlib/plotly/cairo) with graceful-skip behavior when deps are missing
Pin and document compatibility matrix (Python
python-igraph
fastmcp
pydantic) and enforce via CI matrix
Add input validation and sanitization for file I/O endpoints (_construct/_write) including path allowlisting/sandboxing
Split large adapter registration into modular endpoint groups (core
io
layout
drawing
summary) for maintainability
Generate endpoint docs automatically from signatures/docstrings and publish in README_MCP with examples
Add observability hooks (structured logs
per-endpoint latency/error metrics
request IDs)
Introduce caching/memoization for repeat summary/layout calls where safe and invalidated on graph mutation
Add contract tests to detect upstream python-igraph API drift and fail fast when signatures change
Provide async/non-blocking wrappers for long-running operations where MCP server supports concurrency
Add fuzz/property-based tests for graph construction and conversion endpoints to catch edge cases
Use pre-commit in mcp_output (ruff/black/isort/mypy) aligned with parent repo quality gates
Harden packaging by defining explicit console entry points for MCP service startup and healthcheck endpoint
Add security review checklist for exposed methods (DoS vectors
large input bounds
unsafe file access)

success_factors: End-to-end workflow completed with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize)
Import-based adapter strategy was feasible (import_feasibility=0.92), enabling direct wrapping of python-igraph APIs
MCP plugin started successfully and reported healthy service state over stdio transport
Generation pipeline produced all expected service artifacts (entrypoint, adapter, service module, requirements, README) without runtime errors
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking), reducing architecture/context enrichment
Original project test result indicates passed=false with empty details and 0 execution time, suggesting tests were not executed rather than failing
Key metrics were not captured (LLM calls, performance counters, generated LOC/file size reported as 0), limiting diagnostic confidence

node_performance.download_time: Repository ingestion succeeded via zip fallback (179 files discovered). Node appears efficient and reliable for source acquisition.
node_performance.analysis_time: Static analysis completed with medium complexity classification and maintainability index 75; entry-point discovery was sparse but core modules were identified.
node_performance.generation_time: Code generation completed successfully and produced expected MCP structure. However, metadata quality is weak (0 LOC/size/tool_endpoint counts inconsistent with listed endpoints).
node_performance.test_time: Plugin health validation passed, but functional endpoint tests and upstream test suite execution are effectively missing/inconclusive.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0 MB). Instrumentation should be added before concluding efficiency.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0%). Current data suggests metrics collection gap.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate expected footprint; generated artifacts are small, but reported size=0 indicates tracking issue.
